an1.11:0.2: 2. The Chapter on Abandoning the Hindrances
an1.11:1.1: "I see no other single thing, *bhikkhus*, by which sensual desire that has not yet arisen comes to arise, or by which sensual desire that has already arisen grows and expands, as much as this: the beautiful *nimitta*.
an1.11:1.2: For one who attends without appropriate attention [*yoniso manasikāra*] to the beautiful *nimitta*, *bhikkhus*, sensual desire that has not yet arisen comes to arise, and sensual desire that has already arisen grows and expands."
an1.12:1.1: "I see no other single thing, *bhikkhus*, by which ill will that has not yet arisen comes to arise, or by which ill will that has already arisen grows and expands, as much as this: the repulsive *nimitta*.
an1.12:1.2: For one who attends without appropriate attention [*yoniso manasikāra*] to the repulsive *nimitta*, *bhikkhus*, ill will that has not yet arisen comes to arise, and ill will that has already arisen grows and expands."
an1.13:1.1: "I see no other single thing, *bhikkhus*, by which dullness and drowsiness that has not yet arisen comes to arise, or by which dullness and drowsiness that has already arisen grows and expands, as much as this: discontent, listlessness, yawning, heaviness after eating, and sluggishness of mind.
an1.13:1.2: For one whose mind is sluggish, *bhikkhus*, dullness and drowsiness that has not yet arisen comes to arise, and dullness and drowsiness that has already arisen grows and expands."
an1.14:1.1: "I see no other single thing, *bhikkhus*, by which restlessness and remorse that has not yet arisen comes to arise, or by which restlessness and remorse that has already arisen grows and expands, as much as this: the unsettling of the mind.
an1.14:1.2: For one whose mind is unsettled, *bhikkhus*, restlessness and remorse that has not yet arisen comes to arise, and restlessness and remorse that has already arisen grows and expands."
an1.15:1.1: "I see no other single thing, *bhikkhus*, by which doubt that has not yet arisen comes to arise, or by which doubt that has already arisen grows and expands, as much as this: inattention that is not appropriate [*ayonisomanasikāro*].
an1.15:1.2: For one who attends without appropriate attention [*yoniso manasikāra*], *bhikkhus*, doubt that has not yet arisen comes to arise, and doubt that has already arisen grows and expands."
an1.16:1.1: "I see no other single thing, *bhikkhus*, by which sensual desire that has not yet arisen does not arise, or by which sensual desire that has already arisen is abandoned, as much as this: the unattractive *nimitta*.
an1.16:1.2: For one who attends with appropriate attention [*yoniso manasikāra*] to the unattractive *nimitta*, *bhikkhus*, sensual desire that has not yet arisen does not arise, and sensual desire that has already arisen is abandoned."
an1.17:1.1: "I see no other single thing, *bhikkhus*, by which ill will that has not yet arisen does not arise, or by which ill will that has already arisen is abandoned, as much as this: the *mettā* liberation of mind.
an1.17:1.2: For one who attends with appropriate attention [*yoniso manasikāra*] to the *mettā* liberation of mind, *bhikkhus*, ill will that has not yet arisen does not arise, and ill will that has already arisen is abandoned."
an1.18:1.1: "I see no other single thing, *bhikkhus*, by which dullness and drowsiness that has not yet arisen does not arise, or by which dullness and drowsiness that has already arisen is abandoned, as much as this: the element of initiative, the element of endeavor, the element of exertion.
an1.18:1.2: For one of aroused energy, *bhikkhus*, dullness and drowsiness that has not yet arisen does not arise, and dullness and drowsiness that has already arisen is abandoned."
an1.19:1.1: "I see no other single thing, *bhikkhus*, by which restlessness and remorse that has not yet arisen does not arise, or by which restlessness and remorse that has already arisen is abandoned, as much as this: the settling of the mind.
an1.19:1.2: For one whose mind is settled, *bhikkhus*, restlessness and remorse that has not yet arisen does not arise, and restlessness and remorse that has already arisen is abandoned."
an1.20:1.1: "I see no other single thing, *bhikkhus*, by which doubt that has not yet arisen does not arise, or by which doubt that has already arisen is abandoned, as much as this: appropriate attention [*yonisomanasikāro*].
an1.20:1.2: For one who attends with appropriate attention [*yoniso manasikāra*], *bhikkhus*, doubt that has not yet arisen does not arise, and doubt that has already arisen is abandoned."
an1.20:1.4: The second chapter on abandoning the hindrances.
